backend/core/analytics/wave1_subsystem_evidence.py — confirmed on disk. backend/ssot/biomarkers.yaml — confirmed on disk. backend/tests/regression/test_domain_ux1c_governed_subsystem_evidence.py — confirmed on disk. backend/tests/unit/test_wave1_liver_marker_mapping_fix.py — confirmed on disk. backend/tests/regression/test_bilirubin_alias_regression.py — confirmed on disk.

backend/core/analytics/wave1_subsystem_evidence.py:78 — expected_marker_ids=("alp", "albumin", "bilirubin", "total_bilirubin"). total_bilirubin present alongside bilirubin as an independent expected marker. backend/ssot/biomarkers.yaml:1579 — total_bilirubin has display_label_rail_only: true and description: 'Total bilirubin (subsystem/rail display key; canonical lab identity is bilirubin)'. total_bilirubin is not a canonical lab identity and must not appear in expected_marker_ids.

backend/ssot/biomarkers.yaml:1558 — bilirubin is the canonical entry: unit=umol/L, category=Liver Function, system=hepatic. backend/ssot/biomarkers.yaml:1578-1583 — total_bilirubin has display_label_rail_only: true, aliases: []. backend/tests/regression/test_bilirubin_alias_regression.py:30 — EXPECTED_CANONICAL = 'bilirubin'. Fix requires no SSOT policy change.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:123-133 — behavioural scope is explicit: remove total_bilirubin from liver processing expected_marker_ids, ensure bilirubin remains, ensure included/missing partition no longer reports total_bilirubin missing when bilirubin is present. Scope is narrow and bounded to one tuple on one line.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:127-133 — four regression cases required: (1) panel includes bilirubin → total_bilirubin not missing, (2) panel does not include total_bilirubin → no false-missing, (3) liver subsystem does not report total_bilirubin missing when bilirubin available, (4) existing liver markers continue as expected. backend/tests/regression/test_domain_ux1c_governed_subsystem_evidence.py — confirmed on disk as canonical regression target. backend/tests/unit/test_wave1_liver_marker_mapping_fix.py:61-87 — test_wave1_liver_missing_does_not_flag_bilirubin_when_canonical_bilirubin_present already asserts total_bilirubin not in liver.missing_marker_ids at domain level.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:96-118 — out-of-scope list is comprehensive: canonicalisation policy, unit conversion, reference ranges, scoring rails, domain scoring, SignalRegistry, SignalEvaluator, root-cause YAML, PSI, package files, investigation specs, control-plane scripts. Fix requires only: wave1_subsystem_evidence.py:78 (one tuple edit) and test_domain_ux1c_governed_subsystem_evidence.py:125-135 (one test update). No other files required.

backend/core/analytics/wave1_subsystem_evidence.py:75-80 — _WAVE1_LIV_PROCESSING is a static module-level constant; removing total_bilirubin from the tuple is a deterministic change with no runtime branching, no ordering dependency, and no cross-subsystem effect. Expected output change is precisely: total_bilirubin absent from missing_marker_ids in wave1_liv_processing_context when panel contains bilirubin. All other subsystem behaviour unchanged.

backend/tests/regression/test_domain_ux1c_governed_subsystem_evidence.py:125-135 — test_total_bilirubin_emits_governed_display_label asserts 'total_bilirubin' in label_map and label_map['total_bilirubin'] == 'Total Bilirubin'. This test validates the defective state and will fail after the fix. Cursor must replace this test with regression coverage asserting: bilirubin in included_marker_ids when panel contains bilirubin, total_bilirubin not in missing_marker_ids when bilirubin is present. This update is explicitly within allowed scope.

This is a separate layer from the subsystem evidence fix. Workaround must not be modified in this sprint. test_wave1_liver_marker_mapping_fix.py — domain-level tests passing.
